_note: Evergreen David Talley POV lines for text-on-b-roll overlays and standalone brand posts. Compliance-safe (educational, no guarantees or performance claims). Pulled from point-of-view-map.md and voice-guide.md. The overlay matcher pairs a line with a b-roll clip by mood. Add to this over time.

lines.line: The plan should tell your investments what to do, not the other way around.
A number is not a plan.
A retirement answer by itself doesn't remove the worry.
Confidence comes from understanding, not from being told the answer.
Most people don't have too much risk. They have the wrong risk for their plan.
Good advice that nobody implements is just an idea.
You don't need to become the expert. You need a plan you can trust.
In personal finance, two plus two sometimes equals 3.98.
Being stuck in the middle of your own financial life isn't a fair place to be.
Tax planning should care about your lifetime bill, not just this April.
Sometimes the smart move is paying a little more tax this year on purpose.
Complexity usually shows up because something went right.
A projection from software is an input, not a plan.
The most valuable financial decisions are usually sequencing decisions.
Real planning should change what happens next.
Plan first. The investments serve the plan.
You may want a financial planner, not just an advisor.
The goal isn't a bigger number. It's a calmer way to make decisions.
